title: Alanine Transaminase Assay Protocol
author: Peter Havel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ybtfsnn

steps.title: Add 10 μl of each sample...
steps.instruction: Add 10 μl of each sample or standard (in duplicate) to the microplate wells. IMPORTANT: Make sure not to add any bubbles to the wells when dispensing reagents, this will interfere with reading in the platereader.

steps.title: Add 50 μl of ALT Reagent Solution to the wells
steps.instruction: Add 50 μl of ALT Reagent Solution to the wells. Cover wells with the adhesive film and incubate at 37°C for 30 min.

steps.title: Carefully remove adhesive film and add...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove adhesive film and add 50 μl DNPH Color Solution to the wells. Use second film to re-cover wells and incubate for 10 min at 37°C.

steps.title: Remove adhesive and add 200 μl 0
steps.instruction: Remove adhesive and add 200 μl 0.5 M NaOH to each well. Read 510 nm absorbance in plate reader.

steps.title: A standard curve can be constructed...
steps.instruction: A standard curve can be constructed using the serially-diluted standards by plotting the average absorbance for each oxaloacetate standard against its concentration in U/l. Calculate unknowns from standard curve.
